message: I've been working through my family's emergency plan, and I realized how much easier it feels when we've actually talked through the basics together—like where we'd meet if we got separated or how we'd check in on each other. Having a simple plan written down (even just on a shared note in your phone) takes so much of the guesswork out of a stressful moment. If you're looking for a straightforward way to get started, Ready.gov has a solid planning resource that walks you through the actual steps without being overwhelming. What's one part of your family's plan you'd like to nail down first?
